title: About Me
description: Hi! I'm Emily, a computational epidemiologist and data scientist specializing in infectious disease modeling, geospatial analysis, and health disparities. I focus on mechanistic models of infectious diseases with a special focus on rural health and helping prepare for the next pandemic. I received my PhD in Ecology, Evolution & Behavior from UT Austin, where I contributed to COVID-19 pandemic response and genomic studies (GWAS) of osteoarthritis. My work spans academic research, high-performance computing, and applied public health. Always happy to connect on LinkedIn if you're a junior researcher looking for advice/perspective or more senior and want to discuss some science!
